completed_tasks.name: S17-19: Li criterion — Gamma proved, zeta bound = RH, tau_n monotone
S19-20: Connes operator — 6 scripts, W_02/W_p exact, W_R offset = 1.956

remaining_tasks.name: Resolve Connes W_R offset: trace Psi# through kappa/log pullback in Sections 2-3
Write Li criterion preprint (fully completed work, publication-ready)

blockers.blocker: Connes W_R: computed=2.924, correct=0.968, offset=1.956. Formula (4.4) implemented correctly but Psi# convention has a constant shift. Likely in the log pullback D=log*(Psi#) or the kappa isometry.

decisions.decision: Connes W_R offset precisely measured: 1.956 at 100-digit precision
decisions.rationale: W02 and Wp are exact (verified). QW_zeros = W02 - WR - Wp gives WR_correct = 0.968. Our formula (4.4) gives 2.924. The 1.956 offset is in the constant term w_const, not the integral.

decisions.decision: QW null space is 64-dimensional — paper's W_R breaks degeneracy to select unique xi
decisions.rationale: Tested: no QW_zeros eigenvector produces xi_hat zeros near zeta zeros. The W_R contribution is essential for lifting degeneracy. Without correct W_R, no eigenvalue matching is possible.

next_action: SESSION 21 options: (1) Trace Psi# convention through paper Sections 2-3 to resolve the 1.956 offset — this is the key to unlocking the Connes operator. (2) Write Li criterion preprint — clean, completed work. (3) Try the partial-zero approach (K zeros -> predict K+1th) as a publishable Connes result even without full W_R.
context_notes: 16 scripts. Precise diagnostic: WR offset = 1.956 = w_const(our) - w_const(correct) - integral_correction. The w_const = gamma + log(4pi*(e^L-1)/(e^L+1)) = 2.965. Correct total WR = 0.968. The offset is NOT in the integral (which is -0.041), it's in the constant. Paper PDF saved locally. Python: /c/Python313/python.exe.
